judicata: I think K4 is more common than K3
C[judicata: but lately, pros are playing white O17, Q14, R17
C[billywoods: i remember hearing some anonymous chinese guy (who taught 6 dans, so strong) recommend f3 for white 6 here. for reasons i didn't really understand
C[billywoods: but my guess is it's something like: q3 is thick, respond with a thick move.
C[billywoods: i can't stand moyo games. if anyone tries the low chinese i *normally* counter it by punching them in the face.
C[judicata: just most recently, (like, the past few months), pros like the O17 then jump into the corner for white
C[judicata: they used to say it was bad because black gets thick...
C[judicata: i still like white 5 at N4, but it isn't as fun since people are familiar with it :(
billywoods: i don't know it very well, but i've seen it a lot
billywoods: ""black q5, white k4, ??????"" is the extent of my knowledge
C[billywoods: seems kinda bad for white to me. not that i'd know what's good and what's bad, but as white, i'd play it badly, i reckon.
judicata: usual response is Q5, but black can pincer N4 as well, or play P4
C[judicata: i'd say black's opening is successful so far
billywoods: shapes like the lower right are so common, i really should know how to attack / defend them. hmm.
C[judicata: eh, E3 is better than C4
C[judicata: this is still a joseki, but F5 looks stoopid now
judicata: you said the shapes on the lower right are common
judicata: white Q11 will normally be at R11
judicata: in which case R7 is often possible
billywoods: as white, i would have no idea how to attack that best (or even successfully). as black i would have no idea how to respond to, say, white r4 or q6
C[judicata: to r4, I want to say R3
judicata: i remember that response in an older pro game...I want to say with sakata
C[judicata: white invaded with R7, and played the probe around R4...but I can't remember if R4 was before or after.
billywoods: mm. still, the fact that white r4 happened means that there's something behind it, i guess.
C[judicata: the game was commented...i might be able to find it
judicata: I remember it was a probe, and I *think* it was to help the R7 invasion
judicata: so that black had to respond somehow and let white escape
judicata: ah, i was wrong.. not sakata...  shuko
judicata: dang, I was also wrong about R3
billywoods: i have a feeling i have a book about this
billywoods: about joseki that involve invading shimaris, i mean
judicata: It is Yuan Zhao's book
billywoods: this one is takemiya masaki (obviously ghostwritten, it's very averagely written, but a useful reference)
C[judicata: FYI, white had stones at R11 and P11, then white played R7, Black responds at P7 because he can't let white play there, then white played R4, black R5
C[billywoods: does white live in one of those two places?
billywoods: i guess in the corner.
judicata: yeah, the reason R7 worked is because with white at R11, there is a connection tesuji at S9, which I'm sure you know
billywoods: the point of r7 being to force black to split at r5 rather than block the corner at r3, maybe.
judicata: white just leaves R4 after that for aji
judicata: after some fighting, black finally has to Q4, so white takes sente
billywoods: they've taken forever over the last few moves
judicata: yup, :) a pretty easy to understand pro game (as far as that goes, anyway).. it is Shuko's first defense of his Kisei title against Kato Masao
C[billywoods: surely black b11 is a knee-jerk move here, unless it's obviously massively gote :P
C[judicata: i can't imagine it is gote
judicata: white is probably thinking about capturing c11
billywoods: i think i would too
C[judicata: though c5 is pretty big too
billywoods: m11? what a strange move
C[judicata: but I think white should have responded with something like K11 or J11
judicata: splitting M11 and E10 ... but i mean, i dunno
C[billywoods: ooh, i looked away for a second. what's going on on the top
billywoods: oh, i can scroll myself
C[judicata: i'll have to check tomorrow to see how the game turned out

C[djllap: Hello, Have a good game!
C[JohnnyWarpzone: Shit. How did I not see that.
C[JohnnyWarpzone: I was like ""oh man I feel like I'm keeping up with this guy without making too many huge mis- oh god damn it.""
C[djllap: yeah, I felt really behind until I caught J14
JohnnyWarpzone: it's like I grasp hints of the real game at points in time, and then I lose it completely a few hours later
djllap: The middle-game is usually when handicap games are won
JohnnyWarpzone: Honestly I hate playing handicap games
JohnnyWarpzone: yeah sure I get extra stones, woop woop
djllap: The board gets more full, and you have to keep in mind how everything works together and make sure all your stones stay connected.
JohnnyWarpzone: it makes me feel even worse when I lose and if I DO win, I feel like it was the handicap that won it for me
C[djllap: how long have you been playing?
C[JohnnyWarpzone: I don't remember when I played my first game, but it's not been too long
JohnnyWarpzone: I think this is my 15th game or something
C[djllap: I think you are definitely better than 30K
C[djllap: I would guess 17k or so
JohnnyWarpzone: Well I've improved measurably since I started on this site
JohnnyWarpzone: but I've still lost all my games I've played here
JohnnyWarpzone: just, by smaller and smaller margins
JohnnyWarpzone: I thought I had that area locked down
C[JohnnyWarpzone: That started out much better than it ended.
